project.title: N-Glycosylation-Defective IL6 Alternatively Activates the SRC-YAP-SOX2 Axis to Drive Metastasis and EGFR TKI Resistance
project.projectDescription: The IL6-GP130-STAT3 pathway facilitates lung cancer progression and resistance to tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Although glycosylation alters the stability of GP130, its effect on the ligand IL6 remains unclear. We herein found that N-glycosylated IL6, especially at Asn73, primarily stimulates JAK-STAT3 signaling and prolongs STAT3 phosphorylation, whereas N-glycosylation-defective IL6 (deNG-IL6) induces shortened STAT3 activation and alters the downstream signaling preference for the SRC-YAP-SOX2 axis. This signaling shift induced EMT and migration in vitro and metastasis in vivo, which were suppressed by targeted inhibitors and shRNAs against SRC, YAP, and SOX2. Osimertinib-resistant lung cancer cells secreted a large amount of deNG-IL6 through reduced N-glycosyltransferase gene expression, leading to clear SRC-YAP activation. deNG-IL6 contributes to drug resistance, as confirmed by in silico analysis of cellular and clinical transcriptomes and signal expression in patient specimens. Therefore, the N-glycosylation status of IL6 not only affects cell behaviors but also shows promise in monitoring the dynamics of lung cancer evolution.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Preparation of the IL6 protein was performed by the filter-aided sample preparation (FASP) method71. In brief, IL6 (10 g samples) was reduced with 25 mM dithiothreitol and alkylated with 50 mM iodoacetamide in 0.1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.5) with 8 M urea. Afterward, the buffer was replaced with 50 mM tetraethylammonium bromide (TEAB) for protein digestion. A total of 2 µg of glutamyl endopeptidase C (Glu-C) (Promega, V5111) or 1 g of trypsin (Promega, V1651) was utilized for the analysis of IL6 peptides containing Asn73 (N73) or Asn 172 (N172), respectively. Then, N-glycans were removed by treatment with PNGase F. Digested IL6 samples were analyzed by LC–ESI-MS on an Orbitrap Fusion mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) connected to an EASY-nLC 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and EASY-Spray™ Source (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: For glycopeptide identification, Byonic software 4.2.10 (Protein Metrics; RRID:SCR_016735) was used for the identification of summary formulas of glycans associated with glycopeptides (glycan database, 132 entries for N-glycans and 9 entries for O-glycans; precursor mass tolerance, 10 ppm; fragment mass tolerance, 0.5 Da; maximum missed cleavages, 5; and cysteine carbamidomethylation and methionine oxidation)72. The percentage of glycosylation at N73 was determined from the areas in the LC spectra from the peptide sequence TCN73KSNMCE with/without deamidation at N73, and the percentages of glycosylation at N172 and O170 were calculated from the areas in the LC spectra from the peptide sequence NLDAITTPDPT170TN172ASLLTK with/without deamidation at N172.
keywords: Il6; glycosylation; lung cancer; emt; metastasis; egfr-tki resistance; src; yap

references.referenceLine: Hung CH, Wu SY, Yao CD, Yeh HH, Lin CC, Chu CY, Huang TY, Shen MR, Lin CH, Su WC. Defective N-glycosylation of IL6 induces metastasis and tyrosine kinase inhibitor resistance in lung cancer. Nat Commun. 2024 15(1):7885

Comment[Instrument]: Digested IL6 samples were analyzed by LC–ESI-MS on an Orbitrap Fusion mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific) connected to an EASY-nLC 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific) and EASY-Spray™ Source (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: MS/MS scanning was performed in top speed mode with 3 s cycles with CID and HCD
Comment[GradientTime]: the gradient employed was 5% buffer B at 2 min increasing to 60% buffer B at 55 min
